steps.title: Identify the challenge to permanent designations
steps.content: The researchers argue that “invasive” and “native” are temporary labels, not fixed categories. Their point is reinforced by noting that climate change will shift animal ranges and habitat suitability, making current species classifications subject to change.

steps.title: Apply the logic to find the consequence
steps.content: D is the correct answer because it directly follows: if designations are based on temporary circumstances, Namibia may need to reexamine the classification. A predicts a specific outcome not supported by the argument. B invents unrelated past history. C prescriptively changes the label now rather than acknowledging it might require future reconsideration. That matches choice D — D is correct.
